title: Papaver pseudointegrifolium
taxonomy: Papaver pseudointegrifolium is a species of poppy in the family Papaveraceae. It belongs to the genus Papaver, which comprises approximately 50 species of flowering plants. The family Papaveraceae is part of the larger order Ranunculales, which includes various flowering plants. Papaveraceae is further classified under the subclass Rosidae and the class Magnoliopsida. As a member of this family, Papaver pseudointegrifolium shares characteristics with other poppy species and related flowering plants.

intro: Papaver pseudointegrifolium, also known as Himalayan poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in treating various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Papaver pseudointegrifolium include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
